prompt: A practical editorial photograph about safely removing and re-adding one Matter smart-home accessory. Show a homeowner at a warm oak entryway console carefully photographing the small printed setup-code label on a single compact, generic smart plug before resetting it. A modest local smart-home hub and a phone are nearby, clearly secondary. Use a warm lived-in residential setting, natural plaster, oak, linen, one restrained dark-green accent, soft late-morning window light, real textures, and believable everyday imperfection. Keep the hands and smart plug in the central crop-safe area. The setup label may show a small QR-like pattern but no readable numbers or words. No brands, logos, headline, title, watermark, diagrams, charts, network lines, signal waves, decorative tech overlays, glowing nodes, floating UI cards, translucent panels, inset frames, progress bars, split screens, duplicated objects, fake app dashboards, glossy CGI, beige device-diagram style, or generic AI-looking illustration.
